other two badly injured, one dying
on the way to the hospital.


The blow that knocked over st.
Patrick's church wall also leveled
the wall Of the Pacific Mutual Life
building, at Montgomery and Com
mercial, burying several workmen,
one of whom was killed.


The wind was the hardest since
the great fire and rendered condi-
lions in the burnt district exceed
ingly dangerous. Great clouds Of
mortar dust were blown through
the streets, making 1t impossible
to see half a block away.


AID WALL CRASH O.. O.. O..


Two additional bodies were
taken from the ruins of the Pacific
Mutual building this afternoon,
making a total of five deaths. One
man was probably fatally injured
in the Pacific Mutual crash. Two
of the dead weer identified. One
is a Greek named Durant, living in
Oakland, and the other is Peter
Kahn.
